timeAtRisks.description: On-treatment: patients at risk of kidney failure after the third anti-VEGF exposure until the end of continuous drug exposure or the end of the study period
Intent-to-treat sensitivity analysis: all kidney failure events after cohort entry counted regardless of whether the patient was receiving intravitreal anti-VEGF

psSettings.description: Large-scale propensity score with 1:1 propensity score matching

outcomeModels.description: Cox proportional hazards model to estimate the risk of kidney failure while on treatment

timeAtRisks.description: As-treated follow-up: time to first event followed until earliest of end of assigned treatment, last observation, endpoint occurrence, or censoring
Intention-to-treat follow-up: effect of being assigned to treatment regardless of non-adherence

psSettings.description: Variable-ratio propensity score matching with maximum ratio 10 and caliper 0.2

outcomeModels.description: Cox proportional hazards model to estimate hazard ratios and 95% confidence intervals according to exposure

studyPeriods.description: Study period for Longitudinal Patients Database France: 1 January 2012 to 31 December 2019

timeAtRisks.description: TAR 1: On-treatment approach; follow from index date until outcome, treatment discontinuation allowing 90-day gaps, switching to another oral anticoagulant, death, or study end, whichever came first
TAR 2: Intention-to-treat analogue sensitivity analysis; not censored at treatment discontinuation or switching to another anticoagulant

psSettings.description: PS Settings 1: Stratified into 5 propensity score strata to estimate the average treatment effect
PS Settings 2: Propensity score matching with variable matching ratio as sensitivity analysis to estimate the average treatment effect on the treated

outcomeModels.description: Outcome Model 1: Cox proportional hazards regression to estimate hazard ratios for outcomes

timeAtRisks.description: TAR 1: intention-to-treat, from 1 day after admission through 30 days after admission

psSettings.description: PS Settings 1: main analysis stratified into 5 propensity score strata
PS Settings 2: sensitivity analysis using 1:1 propensity score matching

outcomeModels.description: Outcome Model 1: conditional Cox proportional hazards model estimating hazard ratios with treatment as the sole explanatory variable

studyPeriods.description: HIRA COVID-19 RT-PCR testing data collection period

timeAtRisks.description: Patients were censored if they were no longer observable in the database

psSettings.description: Propensity score unadjusted analysis
One-to-four exact propensity score matching with greedy nearest methods
Propensity score stratification with five strata

outcomeModels.description: Cox proportional hazards regression models for time to first outcome occurrence in target relative to comparator cohort pairwise comparisons

studyPeriods.description: Overall study period from initial dabigatran approval to study end

timeAtRisks.description: Primary on-treatment: from 1 day after anticoagulant index date to end of inferred persistent exposure, last observation day, or study end, whichever came first; DOACs allow no more than 3-day gaps plus 5 days appended, warfarin allows no more than 7-day gaps plus 5 days appended

Sensitivity on-treatment: from 1 day after anticoagulant index date to end of inferred persistent exposure, last observation day, or study end, whichever came first; final exposure record allowed no more than 30-day gaps with no days appended for DOACs and warfarin
Intent-to-treat sensitivity: from 1 day after anticoagulant index date until last observation day or study end, whichever came first, regardless of subsequent dispensing records

psSettings.description: Primary propensity score strategy: 1:1 matching of target to comparator patients
Sensitivity propensity score strategy: variable-ratio matching of target to comparator patients up to 1:100

outcomeModels.description: Cox proportional hazards model for time to first outcome occurrence for the target relative to comparator cohort in pairwise comparisons

studyPeriods.description: IUD placements occurring on or after January 1, 2003

timeAtRisks.description: Time at risk from 30 days to 15 years after IUD placement
Subgroup analysis: cervical neoplasm diagnoses at least 1 year after IUD placement, through 15 years

psSettings.description: Propensity score stratification
1:1 propensity score matching

outcomeModels.description: Cox proportional hazards model for cervical neoplasms between Cu IUD and LNG-IUS cohorts

studyPeriods.description: Study period across 10 LEGEND-T2DM real-world data sources, including 6 administrative claims and 4 EHR databases across 4 countries, from 1992 to 2021

timeAtRisks.description: On-treatment time-at-risk from treatment initiation to treatment discontinuation, capturing direct treatment effects while allowing escalation with additional T2DM agents

psSettings.description: Propensity score variable-ratio patient matching to adjust for measured confounding and improve balance between cohorts
Propensity score stratification to adjust for measured confounding and improve balance between cohorts

outcomeModels.description: Cox proportional hazards model to estimate hazard ratios for each outcome and comparison, conditional on propensity score stratification or variable-ratio patient matching

timeAtRisks.description: TAR 1: intention-to-treat (ITT), following patients until the end of data availability
TAR 2: intention-to-treat (ITT) with a 1-year lag
TAR 3: during treatment, from 1 day after the index date until treatment completion, allowing up to a 30-day gap between prescriptions
TAR 4: during treatment with a 1-year lag

psSettings.description: PS Settings 1: 1:1 propensity score matching with a caliper of 0.2 standard deviations of the logit of the propensity score
PS Settings 2: variable-ratio propensity score matching with a maximum ratio of 1:10
PS Settings 3: propensity score stratification into deciles
PS Settings 4: unadjusted

outcomeModels.description: Outcome Model 1: Cox proportional hazards model estimating hazard ratios and 95% confidence intervals according to exposure

studyPeriods.description: Individuals included in COVID-19 vaccine exposure cohorts starting on or after January 1, 2021

timeAtRisks.description: At-risk period 1 to 14 days following vaccination
At-risk period 1 to 28 days following vaccination
At-risk period 1 to 42 days following vaccination
At-risk period 1 to 90 days following vaccination
Anaphylaxis at-risk period 0 to 2 days following vaccination

psSettings.description: Variable ratio matching on the propensity score

outcomeModels.description: Cox proportional hazards model to estimate the hazard ratio for the specified outcome

predJson.name: T2D Medication Use and NAION Risk Study

targetCohort.name: Adults with T2D initiating target medication: semaglutide, dulaglutide, exenatide, empagliflozin, sitagliptin, or glipizide

comparatorCohort.name: Adults with T2D initiating comparator medication: semaglutide, dulaglutide, exenatide, empagliflozin, sitagliptin, or glipizide

timeAtRisks.description: From medication initiation until the end of continuous drug exposure, defined as a gap in exposure of more than 30 days or the end of continuous observation

psSettings.description: 1:1 propensity score matching for each target-comparator exposure comparison

outcomeModels.description: Cox proportional hazards model estimating the hazard ratio of NAION from cohort entry to outcome while taking target and comparator T2D medications; stratified by calendar time periods December 2017-January 2020, February 2020-June 2021, and July 2021-December 2023

studyPeriods.description: Primary study period: included data available from January 1, 2001 through December 31, 2017
Post-hoc study period with study end date redefined as September 30, 2015

timeAtRisks.description: On-treatment time at risk, starting the day after cohort entry and ending at cohort end, with at least 1 day of continuous observation required after time-at-risk start

psSettings.description: Unadjusted crude estimate
Sentinel PS replication strategy: 1:1 matching on propensity score based on 31 Sentinel covariates with caliper of 0.05 on the propensity score scale

Adapted PS strategy: large-scale regularized logistic regression PS using all observed covariates, including recent diagnosis of dementia, with 1:10 variable ratio matching and caliper of 0.2 SD of the logit PS

outcomeModels.description: Cox proportional hazards model estimating hazard ratios during time at risk

studyPeriods.description: Study Period 2: identical study period applied to all databases, March 2013 to December 2016

timeAtRisks.description: Primary analysis: 1-year time at risk, remaining in analysis if allocated drug was discontinued or switched within the first year; censored when no longer observed
Sensitivity analysis: 5-year time at risk
Sensitivity analysis: on-treatment period from 1 day after index date until end of persistent exposure, allowing a 7-day gap, or end of patient record
Sensitivity analysis with 28-day blanking period: 1-year time at risk excluding outcomes in the first 28 days after PCI
Sensitivity analysis with 28-day blanking period: 5-year time at risk excluding outcomes in the first 28 days after PCI
Sensitivity analysis with 28-day blanking period: on-treatment time at risk excluding outcomes in the first 28 days after PCI

psSettings.description: One-to-one greedy propensity score matching
Variable-ratio propensity score matching with maximum ratio of 10
Propensity score stratification using deciles of the propensity score distribution

outcomeModels.description: Cox proportional hazards regression model to estimate the association of exposures with outcomes

timeAtRisks.description: On-treatment time-at-risk from index date of first exposure to end of treatment, allowing up to 30-day gaps between prescriptions
Intent-to-treat time-at-risk from index date until death, database exit, or outcome of interest

psSettings.description: 1:1 propensity score matching using greedy matching with caliper 0.2 standard deviations on the logit scale

outcomeModels.description: Cox proportional hazards model for hazards of O1 or O2 during the time-at-risk

predJson.name: Remicade and NIU population-level estimation study

timeAtRisks.description: On-treatment TAR: day after index until the end of inferred persistent exposure
ITT TAR: day after index until the end of observation time, not censored at discontinuation or other drug exposure

psSettings.description: Primary PS adjustment: 1:10 variable-ratio PS matching with caliper of 0.2 SD on the logit PS scale
Secondary PS adjustment: 1:1 PS matching

outcomeModels.description: Cox proportional hazards model for time to first ever NIU occurrence relative to comparator group
